channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
item.description: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.
item.content:encoded: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: At Mississippi funeral, Civil Rights leaders call for justice for Nolan Wells, 18
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.
item.content:encoded:
The parents of Nolan Wells, who was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi, held a funeral for the 18-year-old on Monday. Wells was with friends for the Fourth of July and did not return home.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Elise Gregg

item.title: What led to the arrests of Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami and now face extradition. New Yorker reporter Heidi Blake talks about the new rape and human trafficking charges from British prosecutors.
item.content:encoded: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami and now face extradition. New Yorker reporter Heidi Blake talks about the new rape and human trafficking charges from British prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: 'Adams Parachute' couldn't save his son, but a grieving father hopes it will help others with addiction
item.description: A father who lost his son to addiction found healing through fly fishing's meditative nature, now he shares it with people recovering from addiction.
item.content:encoded: A father who lost his son to addiction found healing through fly fishing's meditative nature, now he shares it with people recovering from addiction.
item.dc:creator: Craig LeMoult

item.title: 10 things that wowed us at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The United States, Mexico, and Canada co-hosted the nearly six-week tournament. NPR's team of journalists who covered the World Cup reflects on the experience.
item.content:encoded:
The United States, Mexico, and Canada co-hosted the nearly six-week tournament. NPR's team of journalists who covered the World Cup reflects on the experience.

(Image credit: Franck Fife)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
item.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.
item.content:encoded:
They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

(Image credit: Vipin)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: How the Trump administration's new rules could affect international students
item.description: International students could once stay as long as they needed to complete their college degrees, but that's changing, and the restrictions could cut into college budgets.
item.content:encoded: International students could once stay as long as they needed to complete their college degrees, but that's changing, and the restrictions could cut into college budgets.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: New federal law could bring running water to tribal homes, but states have concerns
item.description: More than 10,000 homes on Southwestern Indian reservations lack running water. This despite tribes substantial water rights. A new solution is being held up as states fight over the Colorado River.
item.content:encoded: More than 10,000 homes on Southwestern Indian reservations lack running water. This despite tribes substantial water rights. A new solution is being held up as states fight over the Colorado River.
item.dc:creator: Alex Hager

item.title: America's 250th carries somber note for forcibly displaced Indigenous groups
item.description: Every couple of years members of the Eastern Shawnee Tribe of Oklahoma travel back to visit ancestral and historic sites located in the Midwest. Members say it's especially poignant as the country celebrates the 250th year of American independence.
item.content:encoded: Every couple of years members of the Eastern Shawnee Tribe of Oklahoma travel back to visit ancestral and historic sites located in the Midwest. Members say it's especially poignant as the country celebrates the 250th year of American independence.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Martinez-Smiley

item.title: A cavalcade of international stars headlined the World Cup's first-ever halftime final show
item.description: With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.
item.content:encoded:
With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.

(Image credit: Mauro Pimentel)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas
